title: What are the differences between FetchType.LAZY and FetchType.EAGER in JPA, and when should you use each?

answers.junior: LAZY loads related data only when needed. EAGER loads related data immediately with the parent entity.
answers.middle: LAZY is preferred for large or rarely accessed collections. EAGER is useful for small, frequently used relationships. LAZY reduces memory usage and improves performance.
answers.senior: Default to LAZY for collections, use EAGER only when justified. EAGER can cause performance issues like N+1 selects. Always consider transaction boundaries, as LAZY fields accessed outside a transaction can cause errors.
answers.tricky: Improper use of EAGER can lead to excessive joins and performance bottlenecks. Use DTOs or fetch joins for complex queries. Monitor entity graphs and transaction boundaries to avoid LazyInitializationException.
